title: Implement authentication and authorization for all public-facing API endpoints
description: Add authentication and authorization middleware to all public-facing API endpoints across the Equipment Catalog, Finance, Customer Vetting, Social Media, and RMS services, as well as the Morgan web chat endpoint. Ensure that all /api/v1/* endpoints require valid credentials. Use API key authentication for agent/machine endpoints and session/JWT-based authentication for admin/management endpoints. For the web chat endpoint, implement secure session tokens and rate limiting. Consider a shared authentication middleware or a centralized auth service. Document the authentication flow and update OpenAPI specs.
subtasks: Implement authentication middleware in each service to enforce API key or JWT/session validation for all /api/v1/* endpoints.
Define and document permission levels for endpoints (public, agent, admin).
Integrate session/JWT-based authentication for admin/management endpoints (finance, social, RMS).
Implement WebSocket authentication for the Morgan web chat endpoint using signed tokens.
Add rate limiting and origin validation to the web chat endpoint.
Update OpenAPI documentation to reflect authentication requirements.
Test all endpoints for unauthorized access and ensure proper error responses.
Run OWASP ZAP scan against all public endpoints to verify authentication enforcement.
acceptance_criteria: All /api/v1/* endpoints require valid authentication credentials.
Agent/machine endpoints accept only valid API keys; admin endpoints require valid JWT/session.
Web chat endpoint requires a signed session token and enforces rate limits.
Unauthorized requests receive 401/403 responses with no sensitive data leakage.
OpenAPI docs specify authentication requirements for each endpoint.
OWASP ZAP scan shows no unauthenticated access to sensitive endpoints.

title: Harden Stripe webhook and payment data handling for PCI compliance
description: Enhance the Stripe webhook endpoint and payment data handling in the Finance service to meet PCI DSS SAQ-A requirements. Add request body size limits, rate limiting, and IP allowlisting for the webhook endpoint. Ensure that logs do not contain sensitive payment data. Validate and document PCI compliance posture.
subtasks: Set a strict request body size limit (e.g., 64KB) on the Stripe webhook endpoint.
Implement rate limiting on the webhook endpoint to prevent abuse.
Scrub all logs to ensure no PII or payment identifiers are written.
Validate that the webhook handler is outside normal auth middleware but protected by signature verification and IP allowlisting.
Document and validate PCI DSS SAQ-A compliance posture.
Run a simulated Stripe webhook flood to verify rate limiting and size limits.
Review logs to confirm no sensitive payment data is present.
acceptance_criteria: Stripe webhook endpoint enforces a 64KB request body size limit.
Webhook endpoint is rate limited and only accessible from Stripe IPs.
No sensitive payment data is present in logs.
PCI DSS SAQ-A requirements are documented and met.
Simulated abuse attempts are blocked and logged appropriately.

title: Implement secure encryption and key management for stored OAuth/social tokens
description: Implement application-level encryption for sensitive credentials (OAuth access and refresh tokens) stored in the social_platform_credentials table. Use AES-256-GCM with envelope encryption, store the master key in a secret manager (Vault, AWS KMS), and support key rotation. Document the encryption and key management process.
subtasks: Implement AES-256-GCM encryption for access_token_encrypted and refresh_token_encrypted fields.
Use envelope encryption: encrypt data keys with a master key stored in a secret manager.
Integrate with a secret manager (Vault, AWS KMS) for master key storage and retrieval.
Implement key rotation support with versioned encryption keys.
Update code to decrypt tokens only in memory and never log decrypted values.
Document the encryption and key management process for developers and auditors.
Write integration tests to verify encryption, decryption, and key rotation.
Run a security review to ensure no plaintext tokens are stored or logged.
acceptance_criteria: All OAuth/social tokens are encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM with envelope encryption.
Master encryption key is stored in a secret manager, not in environment variables.
Key rotation can be performed without data loss.
No decrypted tokens are logged or exposed in memory dumps.
Documentation covers encryption, key management, and rotation procedures.

title: Move all credential-bearing connection strings to Kubernetes Secrets and enforce etcd encryption
description: Ensure all database and cache connection strings containing credentials (POSTGRES_URL, REDIS_URL) are stored exclusively in Kubernetes Secrets, not ConfigMaps. Update the ConfigMap to only contain non-sensitive information. Enable etcd encryption at rest for Secrets and document the separation of sensitive and non-sensitive configuration.
subtasks: Audit all ConfigMaps for credential-bearing connection strings and migrate them to Kubernetes Secrets.
Update application deployments to reference Secrets for sensitive data and ConfigMaps for non-sensitive data.
Enable etcd encryption at rest for Kubernetes Secrets using EncryptionConfiguration.
Document the boundary between ConfigMap and Secret usage for all services.
Test that no credentials are present in any ConfigMap.
Run a scan for accidental credential exposure in manifests and pod specs.
acceptance_criteria: No credential-bearing connection strings are present in any ConfigMap.
All sensitive connection strings are stored in Kubernetes Secrets.
etcd encryption at rest is enabled and verified for Secrets.
Documentation clearly defines what belongs in ConfigMaps vs. Secrets.
Credential scan finds no secrets in non-Secret resources.

title: Harden file upload endpoints with comprehensive input validation and abuse protection
description: Enhance the photo/file upload endpoint in the Social Media Engine to include magic byte validation, EXIF/metadata stripping, filename sanitization, rate limiting, and protection against zip bombs or malformed files. Consider antivirus scanning for uploaded files.
subtasks: Implement magic byte validation to verify actual file type matches expected MIME type.
Strip EXIF and metadata from images before storage to remove PII.
Sanitize filenames and ensure S3 keys do not allow path traversal.
Implement per-user/IP rate limiting on uploads.
Set maximum concurrent upload limits and enforce file size limits.
Add protection against zip bombs and malformed image files.
Integrate optional antivirus scanning for uploaded files.
Write tests for all validation and abuse scenarios.
acceptance_criteria: Only valid image files (verified by magic bytes) are accepted.
All uploaded images have EXIF/metadata stripped.
No uploaded file can cause path traversal or overwrite unintended files.
Upload rate limiting and concurrent limits are enforced.
Zip bombs and malformed files are rejected.
Antivirus scan (if enabled) blocks known malware.
All validation and abuse scenarios are covered by tests.

title: Implement tool-level authorization and audit logging for Morgan AI agent tool-server
description: Add fine-grained authorization checks to the MCP tool-server for Morgan, restricting access to sensitive tools based on caller context. Implement audit logging for all tool invocations, recording actor, action, resource, timestamp, and outcome. Ensure audit logs are immutable and written to the audit schema.
subtasks: Define permission levels for all MCP tools (customer, agent, admin).
Implement per-invocation authorization checks in the tool-server.
Restrict sensitive tools (finance, social_publish, admin) to authorized contexts only.
Add audit logging middleware to record all tool invocations with full context.
Write audit events to the audit schema in PostgreSQL as append-only records.
Ensure audit logs are immutable (no UPDATE/DELETE permissions for app roles).
Test tool access with different caller contexts and verify correct enforcement.
Run a review of audit logs to confirm completeness and immutability.
acceptance_criteria: Sensitive tools are only accessible to authorized contexts.
All tool invocations are logged with actor, action, resource, timestamp, and outcome.
Audit logs are append-only and cannot be modified by application roles.
Audit log review shows complete coverage of all sensitive operations.
Unauthorized tool access attempts are denied and logged.

title: Implement PII data governance, encryption, and data retention policies
description: Establish a cross-cutting PII data governance program. Classify all PII fields across schemas, implement column-level encryption for sensitive PII, define data retention and deletion policies, and provide APIs for right-to-erasure and data export. Document the data flow for PII.
subtasks: Inventory and classify all PII fields in CRM, finance, vetting, and quote builder schemas.
Implement column-level encryption for highly sensitive PII (email, phone, payment references).
Define and implement data retention policies with automated cleanup jobs.
Implement a data deletion API to support right-to-erasure (GDPR/CCPA).
Implement a data export API for data portability requests.
Document the PII data flow diagram showing entry, storage, and transmission points.
Test data deletion and export flows for compliance.
acceptance_criteria: All PII fields are classified and documented.
Sensitive PII is encrypted at rest at the column level.
Data retention and deletion policies are enforced automatically.
Right-to-erasure and data export APIs are available and tested.
PII data flow is documented and up to date.

title: Implement explicit input validation and SQL injection protection for all user inputs
description: Add explicit input validation middleware to all services, ensuring all user inputs are sanitized and validated before reaching the repository layer. Enforce parameterized queries in all database access code, especially in the Go RMS service. Add integration tests for SQL injection vectors.
subtasks: Implement input validation middleware in each service for all user-supplied data.
Ensure all database queries use parameterized inputs (sqlx in Rust, pgx Exec/Query with placeholders in Go).
Review and update any queries that use string interpolation.
Add integration tests targeting SQL injection vectors on search and filter endpoints.
Run automated SQL injection testing tools (e.g., sqlmap) against all endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: All user inputs are validated and sanitized before database access.
No queries use string interpolation for user input.
All queries are parameterized and pass static analysis checks.
SQL injection tests do not succeed against any endpoint.
Automated tools report no SQL injection vulnerabilities.

title: Prevent use of placeholder API keys in production and enforce secret validation
description: Implement startup and CI/CD checks to ensure no placeholder API keys (e.g., 'REPLACE_ME_*') are used in production. Integrate with External Secrets Operator if available. Add readiness probes to fail if critical API keys are missing or placeholder.
subtasks: Add startup validation in each service to reject placeholder API keys.
Implement a CI/CD pipeline check that scans secrets for placeholder patterns.
Integrate with External Secrets Operator to pull real credentials from a secret manager.
Add readiness probes that fail if critical API keys are missing or placeholder.
Test deployment with placeholder keys to ensure it fails as expected.
acceptance_criteria: No service can start in production with placeholder API keys.
CI/CD pipeline fails if placeholder secrets are detected.
Readiness probes prevent services from becoming ready with invalid secrets.
External Secrets Operator is used for secret management if available.

title: Integrate container image and dependency vulnerability scanning into CI/CD pipeline
description: Add container image scanning (Trivy, Snyk, or Grype) and dependency vulnerability audit tools (cargo audit, govulncheck, npm/bun audit) to the CI/CD pipeline. Block deployments with critical/high CVEs. Standardize on minimal base images and pin all dependency versions. Set up automated dependency update PRs.
subtasks: Integrate container image scanning into the build pipeline for all services.
Define a policy to block deployment of images with critical/high CVEs.
Standardize Dockerfiles to use minimal base images and pin digests.
Add dependency audit tools for each language ecosystem to the CI pipeline.
Configure Dependabot or Renovate for automated dependency update PRs.
Pin all dependency versions in package manifests.
Schedule recurring scans for deployed images and dependencies.
Test pipeline by introducing a known vulnerable dependency and verifying the build fails.
acceptance_criteria: CI/CD pipeline blocks builds with critical/high CVEs in images or dependencies.
All base images are minimal and pinned by digest.
All dependencies are pinned to exact versions.
Automated update PRs are generated for outdated dependencies.
Recurring scans are scheduled and results are monitored.

title: Implement prompt injection defenses and tool call validation for Morgan AI agent
description: Add a prompt injection defense layer to the Morgan AI agent. Sanitize and flag suspicious inputs, validate tool call parameters, rate limit tool calls per conversation, require confirmation for high-impact operations, and log all suspicious inputs for review.
subtasks: Implement input sanitization to detect and flag common prompt injection patterns.
Add output validation to verify tool call parameters are reasonable before execution.
Rate limit tool calls per conversation to prevent abuse.
Require a confirmation step for high-impact operations (e.g., invoice creation, social publishing).
Sandbox tool execution and validate tool calls against conversation context.
Log all suspicious or flagged inputs for security review.
Test with known prompt injection payloads to verify detection and mitigation.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt injection attempts are detected and blocked or require confirmation.
Tool calls are validated and rate limited per conversation.
High-impact operations require explicit confirmation.
Suspicious inputs are logged for review.
Prompt injection test cases do not result in unauthorized tool execution.
